vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on cron.weekly
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/cron.weekly, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.weekly
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then find -H /etc/cron.weekly/ -maxdepth 0 -perm /u+s,g+xwrs,o+xwrt -type d -exec chmod u-s,g-xwrs,o-xwrt {} \; else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Changing permissions of /etc/cron.weekly to 0700 restricts access to root only, which is a standard hardening step and does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for file_permissions_cron_weekly (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_file_permissions_cron_weekly). Could not verify.

reasoning_messages.reasoning: We need to produce the remediation: change permissions of /etc/cron.weekly to 0700. We have the command: sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.weekly. We must use run_cmd tool with command being the exact command string. So we need to call run_cmd with command: "sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.weekly". Since run_cmd expects a single shell command as root. We'll output the function call with appropriate JSON? Actually we need to output the function call in the specified format: an inner <function=...></function> block must be nested within
reasoning_messages.content:
</tool_call> XML tags. So we need to produce:

<function=run_cmd>
<parameter=command>
sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.weekly
</parameter>
</function>

But must be within <function=...></function>. So:

<function=run_cmd>
<parameter=command>
sudo chmod 0700 /etc/cron.weekly
</parameter>
</function>

Thus final answer.
